mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over trailing months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces the relative attractiveness of cash, driving continuous positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of level.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent as of prior month indicates near-zero policy rate environment
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or negative FEDFUNDS trend periods show flat or negative average forward returns at p > 0.05 significance.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above a 0.3 percentage point increase over a 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that leads earnings expectations lower and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as investors price in slowing consumption.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE recorded at 9.2 percent as of prior month
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.3pp UNRATE rise trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 across two consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising jointly with a low or falling federal funds rate indicate that cheap credit is stimulating durable-goods-linked demand; this joint condition amplifies forward-looking growth expectations and produces stronger positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change positive AND FEDFUNDS below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 560.0 thousand units and FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: monetary easing combined with housing recovery
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform the single-factor HOUST-only or FEDFUNDS-only signal in mean forward 20d return by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05) across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating industrial production growth signals capacity constraints and margin compression ahead, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as forward earnings estimates are revised down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO index at 93.07 as of prior month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating output growth
failure_condition: During INDPRO deceleration episodes identified in late-cycle regimes, forward 60d SPX returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.03) outside late-cycle windows confirming no regime dependency.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon positive momentum in nonfarm payroll employment growth reflects real-time labor demand strength that is quickly incorporated into equity prices, producing a small but consistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following each payroll print.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131017 thousand persons as of prior month
failure_condition: Average forward 5d SPX return following positive PAYEMS month-over-month changes is not statistically distinguishable from average return following negative changes (t-test p > 0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation accelerates above a 2.5% year-over-year threshold, it raises expectations of future policy tightening, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL index at 224.304 as of prior month
failure_condition: Episodes where CPI YoY exceeds 2.5% show forward 20d SPX returns that are positive on average with p < 0.05 significance, contradicting the hypothesized negative relationship, in two or more non-overlapping evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue outlooks, generating a persistent small positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 387789 million dollars as of prior month
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5d SPX return stays below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment alongside a decline in payroll employment growth signals a genuine labor market turning point rather than noise, compounding negative equity risk premium repricing and producing stronger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter than either indicator alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change positive AND PAYEMS 3-month change negative
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.2 percent and PAYEMS at 131017 thousand as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: labor market inflection or recessionary onset
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes do not produce forward 60d returns significantly more negative than the UNRATE-only trigger sample (t-test p > 0.05), indicating no incremental interaction effect.
